nodes.name: stat-index.json
nodes.summary: Graphify tool file-stat cache index mapping absolute file paths to their last-modified timestamps for incremental graph rebuilds.

nodes.name: .terraform-version
nodes.summary: Pins the Terraform CLI version for the railway-cron-drift infrastructure module to ensure reproducible runs.

nodes.name: .terraform.lock.hcl
nodes.summary: Terraform provider lock file for the railway-cron-drift module, locking the Railway provider version and its checksums.
nodes.languageNotes: HCL provider lock files pin provider versions and hash constraints for supply-chain integrity.

nodes.name: railway-cron-poc-plan.rego
nodes.summary: Open Policy Agent Rego policy validating that all Railway cron services in a Terraform plan have the expected cron schedule and environment variables set.

nodes.name: .keep
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file keeping the contracts src directory tracked in git.

nodes.name: community-event.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definition for the CommunityEvent contract, exported from the contracts package for cross-package usage.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the contracts package, re-exporting all public type definitions and database types.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration for the contracts package defining the test environment and file patterns.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the deploy-cli tool defining its binary entry point, dependencies on Railway/Supabase SDKs, and build scripts.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the deploy-cli package extending the shared monorepo tsconfig.

nodes.name: community-event-status.tsx
nodes.summary: React Email component rendering a transactional email for community event status updates, including event details and action buttons.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the email package listing React Email component dependencies and build/deploy scripts.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the email package, re-exporting public email template utilities.

nodes.name: templates.ts
nodes.summary: Maps email template names to their React Email component implementations for use by the email dispatch layer.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the email package with JSX support for React Email components.

nodes.name: drain-queues.mjs
nodes.summary: CLI utility script for draining Supabase queue tables by invoking edge functions and reporting processed item counts.

nodes.name: railway-cron-drift.mjs
nodes.summary: Script that detects configuration drift between expected Railway cron service definitions and live Railway API state, exporting parse and validation utilities.

nodes.name: railway-sandbox.mjs
nodes.summary: Script for provisioning and tearing down Railway sandbox environments for integration testing and ephemeral deployments.

nodes.name: sync-url-validation.mjs
nodes.summary: Script that validates source URLs in the database by checking HTTP reachability and reporting broken or redirected links.

nodes.name: verify-edge-fn.mjs
nodes.summary: Script for smoke-testing deployed Supabase edge functions by invoking them with test payloads and asserting expected responses.

nodes.name: _current_branch
nodes.summary: Supabase CLI marker file recording the currently active database branch name.

nodes.name: p0-003-after.txt
nodes.summary: Benchmark results captured after applying the P0-003 RLS/RPC performance optimization, showing query timing improvements.

nodes.name: p0-003-before.txt
nodes.summary: Benchmark results captured before the P0-003 RLS/RPC performance optimization, serving as the baseline comparison.

nodes.name: p0_003_rls_rpc_benchmarks.sql
nodes.summary: SQL benchmark script measuring Row Level Security policy and RPC function performance for the P0-003 optimization work.

nodes.name: config.toml
nodes.summary: Primary Supabase project configuration defining local development settings, auth providers, edge function auth requirements, and storage buckets.
